growth_records.strain: M. extorquens AM1 wild type tagged with mCherry fluorescent protein
growth_records.medium: Acetate minimal medium
growth_records.cite: Lee et al. Rapid formation of bioaggregates and morphology transition to biofilm streamers induced by pore-throat flows. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2204466120. [PMC10083537]
growth_records.snippet: The composition of the acetate minimal media is as follows: 2.53 g/L K2HPO4, 2.25 g/L NaH2PO4, 0.54 g/L Na2SO4, 0.10 g/L MgSO4, 0.003 g/L CaCl2, 0.68 g/L acetate, 0.8 g/L methylamine, and 1 mL of metal mix in a 1 L of the medium.
growth_records.notes: Record captured for full defined-medium composition (acetate minimal, fully itemized). No growth rate or doubling time reported for M. extorquens AM1 in this paper (the 2.8 h doubling time is attributed to the E. coli growth experiment). growth_rate/doubling_time null. Prokaryote (Alphaproteobacteria) grown at 25 C under shaking.

growth_records.medium: Hypho minimal medium (HY), methanol sole carbon, 1.35 uM cobalt

up.method: derived qMeOH=mu/Yxs; specific methanol uptake also measured directly in exp phase (absolute value in Suppl. Tables S2-S5)

mfa.note: derived from mu/Yxs; formate node partitioned between FDH (->CO2) and FTHFL (->assimilation); FTHFL carries 36% more normalized flux in LL than VL in HY
LL 91% higher than VL in HY; malic enzyme (ME) LL 97% higher than VL in HY; PK/PYRDH higher in VL (rate-associated)

growth_records.cite: Fu et al. Difference in C3-C4 metabolism underlies tradeoff between growth rate and biomass yield in Methylobacterium extorquens AM1. BMC Microbiology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12866-016-0778-4. [PMC4949768]
growth_records.snippet: LL + HY 8.90 +/- 0.59 (g cdw/mol methanol) ... 0.09 +/- 0.01 (per hr). Excretion products were tested in culture supernatant and were found to be negligible.
growth_records.notes: Table 1 base case. mu=0.09/h, Yxs=8.90 g DCW/mol methanol (both reported). qMeOH=mu/Yxs=0.09/8.90=0.010112 mol/gDW/h=10.11 mmol/gDW/h (derived; paper states absolute qMeOH measured in exp phase, values in Suppl.). td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.09=7.70 h. Excretion negligible. 13C-MFA paper (VL/LL variants, cobalt effect).

up.method: derived qMeOH=mu/Yxs; absolute measured qMeOH in Suppl.

mfa.note: VL has higher absolute methanol uptake and formate flux than LL; higher PK/PYRDH flux (growth-rate route)

growth_records.cite: Fu et al. Difference in C3-C4 metabolism underlies tradeoff between growth rate and biomass yield in Methylobacterium extorquens AM1. BMC Microbiology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12866-016-0778-4. [PMC4949768]
growth_records.notes: Table 1 base case. mu=0.13/h, Yxs=6.44 g DCW/mol methanol (reported). qMeOH=0.13/6.44=0.020186 mol/gDW/h=20.19 mmol/gDW/h (derived). td=0.693/0.13=5.33 h. VL grows 22% faster but 28% lower yield than LL.

growth_records.cite: Fu et al. Difference in C3-C4 metabolism underlies tradeoff between growth rate and biomass yield in Methylobacterium extorquens AM1. BMC Microbiology (2016). DOI:10.1186/s12866-016-0778-4. [PMC4949768]
growth_records.snippet: LL + HYC 6.09 +/- 0.04 (g cdw/mol methanol) ... 0.13 +/- 0.01 (per hr). The LL strain grew 26 % faster but with 32 % lower biomass yield in HYC medium than in HY medium
growth_records.notes: Table 1 base case. mu=0.13/h, Yxs=6.09 g DCW/mol methanol (reported). qMeOH=0.13/6.09=0.021346 mol/gDW/h=21.35 mmol/gDW/h (derived). td=0.693/0.13=5.33 h. Higher cobalt (6.31 uM) raises rate, lowers yield vs HY.

growth_records.medium: Minimal medium (succinate-limited)

up.method: qS = D*S_feed/X; D=0.163 h-1, S_feed=3.7 mM (growth-limiting, residual ~0), X=OD600 0.63 * 0.278 mg/mL/OD = 0.1751 g/L; qS = 0.163*3.7/0.1751 = 3.44

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: carbon flux to biomass (succinate-limited steady state)
mfa.note: Reported value, calculated from the dilution rate; equals ~1.8 mmol C/L/h = ~10.3 mmol C/gDW/h (X=0.1751 g/L). Dropped to ~0.2 nmol C/min/mL upon shift to methanol.

Estimated that ~50% of the carbon in biomass comes from assimilated CO2 (from 14C elimination in high unlabeled CO2); biomass is 47% carbon by weight; 0.278 mg DW per mL per OD600=1.0 (cited ref [21]).

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: methanol -> formaldehyde+formate+CO2 (total oxidative flux, 2 h post methanol-shift, batch)
mfa.note: Transient batch transition after adding 50 mM methanol to succinate chemostat cells; ~3/4 of the full methanol-grown flux of 41.5 nmol/min/[mL at 1 OD] which is CITED from Crowther et al. ref [21], not measured here.

yields.note: Yxs = X/succinate_fed = 0.1751 g/L / (3.7 mM * 118.09 g/mol = 0.437 g/L) = 0.40 gDW/g succinate; assumes full consumption of growth-limiting succinate.

growth_records.cite: Skovran, Elizabeth et al. A Systems Biology Approach Uncovers Cellular Strategies Used by Methylobacterium extorquens AM1 During the Switch from Multi- to Single-Carbon Growth. PLoS ONE (2010). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0014091. [PMC2991311]
growth_records.snippet: M. extorquens AM1 was cultivated in a minimal medium containing 3.7 mM succinate as a growth-limiting nutrient in a 2.2-liter bench-top BioFlo 110 Modular Fermentor with a 1 liter working volume, resulting in an OD 600 of ~0.63. Dilution ra
growth_records.notes: Only genuine experimental prokaryote steady-state condition in the 4-paper batch. mu = D = 0.163 h-1 at steady state (calculated); doubling time = ln2/0.163 = 4.25 h. qS(succinate) = D*S_feed/X = 0.163*3.7/0.1751 = 3.44 mmol/gDW/h, using X = OD600 0.63 * 0.278 mg/mL = 0.1751 g/L (0.278 mg DW/mL/OD cited from ref [21]). Yxs = 0.1751/0.437 = 0.40 gDW/g. Full medium salt composition not stated (cited ref [14]); pH of culture not reported (28 C stated; quench buffer pH 6.8 is not culture pH). No product secretion at succinate steady state; transient excretion of formaldehyde (~50 uM peak) and formate (~450 uM peak) occurred only during the dynamic methanol-shift (non-steady-state, not tabulated as a rate). Full methanol-grown oxidative flux 41.5 nmol/min/[mL at 1 OD] is CITED from Crowther et al. ref [21], not measured in this study. NOTE: other 3 papers in batch yielded NO prokaryote experimental record: PMC5697012 (M. tuberculosis) is purely in-silico FBA/PhPP with no measured rates; PMC8048565 (S. cerevisiae) and PMC1794586 (S. cerevisiae) are eukaryotic yeast, out of prokaryote scope.

growth_records.strain: G4105 (TK 0001-derived, adapted to 5% methanol) carrying empty plasmid pTE102 (control)
growth_records.medium: Standard mineral (SM) medium (DSMZ n°1629) + 5% (v/v) methanol
growth_records.cite: Belkhelfa, Sophia et al. Continuous Culture Adaptation of Methylobacterium extorquens AM1 and TK 0001 to Very High Methanol Concentrations. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01313. [PMC6595629]
growth_records.snippet: the metY-expressing cells grew more slowly, with a gain in doubling time of 22% (from 9h40 to 12h25) at 5% methanol
growth_records.notes: Control = adapted strain G4105 with empty plasmid pTE102. Doubling time 9h40 = 9+40/60 = 9.667 h (reported in text for Figure 6). mu calculated: mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/9.667 = 0.0717 h^-1. No uptake/secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h reported in this paper; lactate data are intracellular pool sizes (LC-HRMS EIC), not secretion fluxes. Methanol 5% v/v: 50 mL/L x 0.792 g/mL = 39.6 g/L, /32.04 g/mol = 1.24 M (paper corroborates: '7% methanol (1.7 M)'). SM medium full composition not given (DSMZ n1629 reference only).

growth_records.strain: G4980 (G4105 carrying pTE102_metY, overexpressing wildtype O-acetyl-L-homoserine sulfhydrylase MetY)
growth_records.medium: Standard mineral (SM) medium (DSMZ n°1629) + 5% (v/v) methanol
growth_records.cite: Belkhelfa, Sophia et al. Continuous Culture Adaptation of Methylobacterium extorquens AM1 and TK 0001 to Very High Methanol Concentrations. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01313. [PMC6595629]
growth_records.snippet: the metY-expressing cells grew more slowly, with a gain in doubling time of 22% (from 9h40 to 12h25) at 5% methanol ... biomass production of G4980 cells was lowered by about 26% at this high methanol concentration as compared with the cont
growth_records.notes: G4980 = G4105 + pTE102_metY. Doubling time 12h25 = 12+25/60 = 12.417 h (reported in text for Figure 6). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/12.417 = 0.0558 h^-1. This is 22% higher td than control (matches paper's stated +22%). Biomass yield reduced ~26% vs control at 5% methanol (relative, no absolute Yxs given). No uptake/secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h reported; lactate measured as intracellular pool only. Full SM medium composition not stated.

growth_records.strain: AM1, engineered strain 'EM' (GSH-dependent formaldehyde oxidation replacing native H4MPT pathway; CM701 pink / CM702 white)
growth_records.medium: methanol minimal medium

up.method: sole carbon source; uptake rate not reported

growth_records.cite: Marx et al. Recovering from a bad start: rapid adaptation and tradeoffs to growth below a threshold density. BMC Evolutionary Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1471-2148-12-109. [PMC3495640]
growth_records.snippet: Preliminary experiments established that the EM strains could maintain themselves under this regime if begun at the typical final density of ~1 x 10^8 mL-1, as expected from their growth rate (0.062 +/- 0.001 h-1; SE reported throughout; do
growth_records.notes: Engineered M. extorquens AM1 'EM' on methanol minimal medium. Reported specific growth rate 0.062 +/- 0.001/h, doubling 11.1 h (measured; consistent: ln2/11.1 = 0.0624/h). Medium named 'methanol minimal medium' but composition not given in text. No uptake/secretion rates measured (this is an experimental-evolution / density-threshold study).

growth_records.medium: methanol minimal medium

up.method: sole carbon source; uptake rate not reported

growth_records.cite: Marx et al. Recovering from a bad start: rapid adaptation and tradeoffs to growth below a threshold density. BMC Evolutionary Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1471-2148-12-109. [PMC3495640]
growth_records.snippet: The resulting strain [EM] recovers the ability to grow on C1 compounds, but does so three times more slowly than wild-type ... EM grows three-fold worse than wild-type in methanol
growth_records.notes: Wild-type M. extorquens AM1 growth rate DERIVED, not directly stated. EM measured mu = 0.062/h and EM 'grows three times more slowly than wild-type', so WT mu = 3 x 0.062 = 0.186/h; doubling = ln2/0.186 = 3.7 h. Low confidence: derived from a qualitative '3-fold' statement, not a directly reported wild-type rate.

derived.qs_mu_note: pooled across studies/media — not a single chemostat series, so no maintenance model fitted
